title: alangium villosum subsp. pilosum
intro: Alangium villosum subsp. pilosum, also known as the hairy alangium, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. Alangium villosum subsp. pilosum is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing pain, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Alangium villosum subsp. pilosum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional remedies in the form of poultices, salves, or ointments.
